title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Raymond Weitekamp examines Recursive Language Models and how their inference-time compute patterns can be applied to coding agents. The approach aims to improve performance and reliability in agent behavior.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse examines Recursive Language Models and how their inference-time compute patterns can be applied to coding agents. The talk connects test-time compute techniques to agent design, with the goal of improving performance and reliability in agent behavior. The central claim is that recursive inference patterns are a lever for more dependable coding agents.
